---
title: 'The Kingdom of God: The life and work of Jesus, salvation, and the birthing of the Church.'
date: '2021-09-23T03:48:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: false
categories:
  - 'Studies'
  - 'Class Assignment'
  - 'Biblical Studies'
  - 'Armchair Theology'
---

<span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">&nbsp;</span>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">&nbsp;</span></p>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;"><span class="MsoBookTitle"><span style="font-size: 16.0pt; line-height: 107%;">The Kingdom
of God: The life and work of Jesus, salvation, and the birthing of the Church.</span></span></p><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">&nbsp;</span>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">&nbsp;</span></p>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The
King’s University, Southlake, Texas</span></p>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">New
Testament Studies (BIBL1306)</span></p>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Professor:
Jonathan E Jennings MTS</span></p>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">April
4, 2021</span></p>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">&nbsp;</span></p>

<p align="center" class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-align: center;"><span style="font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 200%; mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">By
Darrell Wolfe</span></p>

<p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p>

<p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p>

<h1 style="margin: 0in;">The Kingdom of God: Jesus’ Life Mission and Message</h1>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; margin-bottom: 0in; text-indent: .5in;"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The mission and message of Jesus
can be wrapped up in the phrase “He came preaching and teaching The Kingdom of
God”.<a href="#_ftn1" name="_ftnref1" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn1;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[1]</span></span></span></span></a> So,
what is this kingdom?<span style="mso-spacerun: yes;">&nbsp; </span>Approximately one
third (or 32 percent) of the New Testament is built from quotes of the Old
Testament (OT).<a href="#_ftn2" name="_ftnref2" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn2;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[2]</span></span></span></span></a>
A Rabbi himself, Jesus “strings pearls” by using Old Testament quotes more
often than most western Christians realize.<a href="#_ftn3" name="_ftnref3" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn3;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[3]</span></span></span></span></a> A
review of the macro-story of the Old Testament shows that God’s Dominion (or
Kingdom) is consistently pitted against those of other nation’s gods (like Baal
and Dagon). The OT promises echo-forward to a time when God’s divine King would
come and restore all things. By the time Jesus arrived, driven largely by the
book of Daniel, many in Israel were already expecting The Kingdom of God to
come and restore Israel’s fortunes.<a href="#_ftn4" name="_ftnref4" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn4;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[4]</span></span></span></span></a></span></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; margin-bottom: 0in; text-indent: .5in;"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">In the Gospels, Matthew shows us
that Jesus is the Davidic Messiah and the Kingdom of God is realized in His
arrival.<a href="#_ftn5" name="_ftnref5" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn5;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[5]</span></span></span></span></a> Mark
shows us the “Now and Not Yet” of this Kingdom.<a href="#_ftn6" name="_ftnref6" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn6;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[6]</span></span></span></span></a>
John wanted to make it so clear that “God So Loved The World” that he used the
word “world” seventy-eight times in his Gospel.<a href="#_ftn7" name="_ftnref7" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn7;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[7]</span></span></span></span></a>
John demonstrated that the life (Tree of Life) that was lost at Eden was
restored through Jesus, who was the fulfillment of the types and shadows of the
Old Covenant, and that Jesus was the start of a New Covenant which included the
nations (world) lost at Babel.<a href="#_ftn8" name="_ftnref8" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn8;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[8]</span></span></span></span></a> Luke
emphasized that Jesus came to “seek and save what was lost” (19:10) and was the
only Gospel writer to use the Greek words for Salvation and Savior.<a href="#_ftn9" name="_ftnref9" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn9;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[9]</span></span></span></span></a> <span style="mso-spacerun: yes;">&nbsp;</span>He showed that Jesus came to redeem the world
of sin so that we could enter the Kingdom of God and inherit “eternal life” or
God-quality-life.<a href="#_ftn10" name="_ftnref10" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn10;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[10]</span></span></span></span></a><span style="mso-spacerun: yes;">&nbsp; </span>The theme of God as source of all life is
echoed throughout the Old Testament and finds its crescendo in the Eternal Life
(Zoe Life) found in Jesus.<a href="#_ftn11" name="_ftnref11" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn11;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[11]</span></span></span></span></a></span></p>

<h1 style="margin: 0in;">The Birthing of The Church</h1>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; margin-bottom: 0in; text-indent: .5in;"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Luke then takes us a step farther
in his part two. In Acts, the Holy Spirit (third member of the YHWH Godhead) storms
onto the scene.<a href="#_ftn12" name="_ftnref12" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn12;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[12]</span></span></span></span></a>
If the Father is the star of the Old Testament, and Jesus (the Son) is the star
of the Gospels, the Holy Spirit is the star of Acts; ushering in the “fruitful
and multiply” component of the Kingdom of God. Every major plot-movement in the
book of Acts is driven by the work of the Holy Spirit himself.<a href="#_ftn13" name="_ftnref13" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn13;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[13]</span></span></span></span></a>
Luke-Acts could be considered both a blue-print for building the church and for
discipleship.<a href="#_ftn14" name="_ftnref14" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn14;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[14]</span></span></span></span></a></span></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; margin-bottom: 0in; text-indent: .5in;"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Peter gives the first sermon and
3,000 Jews were restored into The Kingdom of God (aka the Family of God) on the
first day; birthing The Church (Ekklesia, a political-governing term).<a href="#_ftn15" name="_ftnref15" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn15;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[15]</span></span></span></span></a>
God’s Dominion was being declared pre-eminent over Planet Earth once again, and
these were his Kingdom Ambassadors. Then Paul joins the Kingdom and for the
first time since Babel, the nations begin being restored to God’s Dominion.</span></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; margin-bottom: 0in; text-indent: .5in;"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Among the key pinch-points in
Acts is the battle between “Judaizers” on one side and “Pagans” on the other.<a href="#_ftn16" name="_ftnref16" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn16;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[16]</span></span></span></span></a> On
one hand, Judaizers threaten to return God’s People to a dead-works style of
doing a better “Knowledge of Good”; which was rooted in the Tree of Knowledge,
which was the root of the death of Eden in the first place. On the other hand,
they are constantly pressured to placate the people of other nations who
worship other gods (The Pseudo-Kingdom of Darkness). </span></p>

<h1>Conclusion</h1>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; margin-bottom: 0in; text-indent: .5in;"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">God (YHWH) consistently demands
believing loyalty from his Kingdom Citizens; a theme throughout the Old
Testament and only strengthened in Acts. When God’s people demonstrate their
loyalty preaching the Kingdom, God confirms his word with signs and wonders.<a href="#_ftn17" name="_ftnref17" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn17;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[17]</span></span></span></span></a>
Even though it may lead to their death in this life, Kingdom People keep
showing The World that God’s Kingdom reigns over all, calling them home.</span></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; margin-bottom: 0in; text-indent: .5in;"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">One day, The King shall return.</span></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; margin-bottom: 0in;"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">&nbsp;</span></p>

<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 11.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;"><br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" />
</span>

<p class="MsoNormal"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">&nbsp;</span></p>

<h1>Bibliography</h1>

<p class="MsoNormal">&nbsp;</p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Berding, Kenneth, and Matt
Williams. <i>What the New Testament Authors Really Cared about: A Survey of
Their Writings</i>. Second Edition. Grand Rapids Mich.: Kregel Publications,
2008.</span></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Chilton,
Bruce. <i>Pure Kingdom: Jesus’ Vision of God</i>. Studying the Historical
Jesus. Grand Rapids, Mich. : London: Eerdmans ; Society for Prommoting
Christian Knowledge, 1996.</span></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Heiser,
Michael. <i>The Unseen Realm: Recovering the Supernatural Worldview of the
Bible</i>. First edition. Bellingham, WA: Lexham Press, 2015.</span></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Hill,
Andrew E., and John H. Walton. <i>A Survey of the Old Testament</i>. 3rd ed.
Grand Rapids, Mich: Zondervan Publishing House, 2009.</span></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Jennings,
Jon. “New Testament Studies (BIBL1306).” Lectures, The King’s University,
Southlake Texas, Spring 2021.</span></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><i><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">NET
Bible®New English Translation (NET)</span></i><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">. Online Notes Edition. HarperCollins Christian Publishing;
Biblical Studies Press, L.L.C., n.d. https://netbible.com/copyright/.</span></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Perrin,
Nicholas, Jeannine K. Brown, and Joel B. Green. <i>Dictionary of Jesus and the
Gospels (DJG)</i>. IVP Bible Dictionary Series, K is for Kingdom; Judaism,
Common. Downers Grove, Ill: IVP Academic, 2013. http://search.ebscohost.com/login.aspx?direct=true&amp;db=nlebk&amp;AN=633424&amp;site=ehost-live.</span></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Spangler,
Ann, and Lois Tverberg. <i>Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith</i>. Updated edition. Grand
Rapids, Michigan: Zondervan, 2018.</span></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Wright,
N. T., and Michael F. Bird. <i>The New Testament in Its World: An Introduction
to the History, Literature, and Theology of the First Christians</i>. London :
Grand Rapids, MI: SPCK ; Zondervan Academic, 2019.</span></p>

<p class="MsoNormal"><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">&nbsp;</span></p>

<div style="mso-element: footnote-list;"><br clear="all" />

<hr align="left" size="1" width="33%" />



<div id="ftn1" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref1" name="_ftn1" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn1;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[1]</span></span></span></span></a>
<i><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">NET
Bible®New English Translation (NET)</span></i><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">, Online Notes Edition
(HarperCollins Christian Publishing; Biblical Studies Press, L.L.C., n.d.),
Matthew 4:23 Paraphrase, https://netbible.com/copyright/.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn2" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref2" name="_ftn2" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn2;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[2]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Andrew
E. Hill and John H. Walton, <i>A Survey of the Old Testament</i>, 3rd ed (Grand
Rapids, Mich: Zondervan Publishing House, 2009), 744–45.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn3" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref3" name="_ftn3" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn3;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[3]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Ann
Spangler and Lois Tverberg, <i>Sitting at the Feet of Rabbi Jesus: How the
Jewishness of Jesus Can Transform Your Faith</i>, Updated edition (Grand
Rapids, Michigan: Zondervan, 2018), Chapter: Stringing Pearls.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn4" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref4" name="_ftn4" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn4;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[4]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Bruce
Chilton, <i>Pure Kingdom: Jesus’ Vision of God</i>, Studying the Historical
Jesus (Grand Rapids, Mich. : London: Eerdmans ; Society for Prommoting
Christian Knowledge, 1996), 25.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn5" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref5" name="_ftn5" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn5;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[5]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Kenneth
Berding and Matt Williams, <i>What the New Testament Authors Really Cared
about: A Survey of Their Writings</i>, Second Edition (Grand Rapids Mich.:
Kregel Publications, 2008), 37; 41–43.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn6" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref6" name="_ftn6" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn6;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[6]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Berding
and Williams, 75–77.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn7" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref7" name="_ftn7" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn7;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[7]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Berding
and Williams, 122–23.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn8" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref8" name="_ftn8" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn8;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[8]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Berding
and Williams, 121–42; Michael Heiser, <i>The Unseen Realm: Recovering the
Supernatural Worldview of the Bible</i>, First edition (Bellingham, WA: Lexham
Press, 2015), Concept of restoring the nations lost at Babel found in this
work.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn9" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref9" name="_ftn9" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn9;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[9]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Berding
and Williams, <i>What the New Testament Authors Really Cared About</i>, 89.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn10" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref10" name="_ftn10" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn10;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[10]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Berding
and Williams, 90–91.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn11" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref11" name="_ftn11" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn11;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[11]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Nicholas
Perrin, Jeannine K. Brown, and Joel B. Green, <i>Dictionary of Jesus and the
Gospels (DJG)</i>, IVP Bible Dictionary Series, K is for Kingdom; Judaism,
Common (Downers Grove, Ill: IVP Academic, 2013), LIFE, ETERNAL LIFE,
http://search.ebscohost.com/login.aspx?direct=true&amp;db=nlebk&amp;AN=633424&amp;site=ehost-live.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn12" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref12" name="_ftn12" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn12;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[12]</span></span></span></span></a>
<i><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">NET
Bible®</span></i><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">, Acts 2.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn13" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref13" name="_ftn13" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn13;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[13]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Jon
Jennings, “New Testament Studies (BIBL1306)” (Lectures, The King’s University,
Southlake Texas, Spring 2021), Lecture on Acts.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn14" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref14" name="_ftn14" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn14;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[14]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">N. T.
Wright and Michael F. Bird, <i>The New Testament in Its World: An Introduction
to the History, Literature, and Theology of the First Christians</i> (London :
Grand Rapids, MI: SPCK ; Zondervan Academic, 2019), 644–45.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn15" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref15" name="_ftn15" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn15;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[15]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Perrin,
Brown, and Green, <i>DJG</i>, “Church.”</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn16" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref16" name="_ftn16" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn16;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[16]</span></span></span></span></a>
<span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">Wright
and Bird, <i>The New Testament in Its World</i>, 109.</span></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn17" style="mso-element: footnote;">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText" style="text-indent: .5in;"><a href="#_ftnref17" name="_ftn17" style="mso-footnote-id: ftn17;" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="mso-special-character: footnote;"><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-bidi-language: AR-SA; mso-bidi-theme-font: minor-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-fareast;">[17]</span></span></span></span></a>
<i><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">NET
Bible®</span></i><span style="mso-bidi-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-bidi-font-size: 12.0pt;">, Mark 16:20; Acts 2:22, 2:43; 4:16-30; 5:12; 14:3; 15:12;;
Wright and Bird, <i>The New Testament in Its World</i>, 113–15.</span></p>

</div>

</div>
